page_title: Zanzibar Wellness Retreats & Yoga · Destination Paradise
meta_description: Join teacher-led Zanzibar wellness retreats with yoga, bodywork, ocean-view practice, island excursions, and fixed departures.

hero.subtitle: Teacher-led yoga, Thaivedic bodywork, coastal reset weeks, and safari journeys.
hero.lead: Choose a fixed group retreat or ask us to shape a private teacher-led program around your dates, pace, and practice.

intention.text: A retreat can be a reset, a deep practice week, a softer landing after safari, or the heart of a longer Zanzibar journey. We build each one around real teachers, generous time, and the kind of island days that let people breathe again.

retreat_types.title: Choose the rhythm that fits.
retreat_types.lead: Start with one of our ready-made retreat formats, then let us tune the dates, teacher mix, excursions, rooms, and safari extensions around your group.

items.title: Yoga & Safari Journey
items.text: One shared retreat journey with three ways to join: the full Zanzibar and safari experience, the Zanzibar practice days only, or the mainland safari block only.
items.image_alt: Yoga practice above the Zanzibar coastline

items.title: Coastal Reset Week
items.text: A lighter wellness week for travelers who want daily practice, ocean time, bodywork, nourishing meals, and optional island excursions.
highlights: Beach, reef, and Stone Town add-ons
items.image_alt: Awham guiding a supported leg stretch beside a Lamu pool

items.title: Awham Thaivedic Flow Retreat
items.text: A Lamu-inspired retreat built around Awham's all-level vinyasa, hatha, Thaivedic bodywork, hands-on adjustments, and slow coastal recovery.
items.image_alt: Awham guiding Thaivedic bodywork on a yoga mat

items.title: Private Teacher-Led Retreat
items.text: A tailor-made retreat for couples, friends, companies, or teachers bringing their own group to Zanzibar.
highlights: Practice lead matched to the group
Private villa or boutique hotel base
items.image_alt: Awham adjusting a student in a private studio session

teachers.title: Several teachers, one retreat rhythm.
teachers.lead: Every retreat is teacher-led. Depending on the format, the program can combine Nina's mindful yoga, Awham's all-level vinyasa and Thaivedic bodywork, plus guest practitioners and local hosts.
teachers.selection_lead: Click a teacher above to see the retreat formats connected to their practice, then open the booking request with that retreat already selected.

detail.focus_lead: Use this page to understand the teacher's practice focus, then choose one of the connected retreat formats below.
detail.retreats_lead: These are the retreat formats currently connected to this teacher. Each one opens the booking request with the right retreat selected.
detail.cta_text: Share your dates, group size, practice level, and whether you want the program to stay coastal or connect with safari.
detail.not_found_page_title: Retreat teacher not found · Destination Paradise
detail.not_found_title: That retreat teacher is not in the current program.
detail.not_found_text: Browse the current retreat teachers and choose the profile you want to explore.

items.name: Nina Pfatischer
items.text: Nina leads warm, mindful practices shaped by teacher training in Portugal and teaching experience in Morocco and Germany. Her sessions bring movement, breath, music, philosophy, and quiet integration into the retreat rhythm.
items.image_alt: Group yoga practice with a teacher leading the room

items.name: Awham Mohamed Al-Obaidy
items.text: Lamu born and raised, Awham is an international yoga and Thaivedic expert, content creator, and wellness-hospitality storyteller. He brings vinyasa, hatha, and hands-on bodywork into retreat programs with an ocean-life sensibility.
items.image_alt: Awham adjusting a student's downward dog in his Lamu studio

items.name: Guest teachers & island hosts
items.text: Depending on the retreat, the team can include local wellness practitioners, guides, chefs, massage therapists, safari planners, and island hosts who keep the journey grounded, practical, and connected to place.
items.image_alt: Small group practicing yoga by the beach

quote.text: The best retreat does not ask everyone to move the same way. It creates enough structure to feel held, and enough space to return to yourself.

practice.title: Movement, stillness, and bodywork.
practice.lead: The retreat program can lean energetic, restorative, therapeutic, or expedition-led depending on the teacher and the group.

items.title: Vinyasa for all levels
items.text: Dynamic, breath-led sequences with options for beginners, returning students, and experienced practitioners.

items.title: Hatha Yoga
items.text: Mindful alignment, conscious breathing, and steady practice that builds awareness without rushing the body.

items.title: Yin & restorative evenings
items.text: Long-held postures, soft props, meditation, and deep release after ocean days or safari travel.

items.title: Thaivedic bodywork
items.text: Hands-on therapeutic sessions that support recovery, nervous-system rest, and a more grounded retreat experience.

journey.title: From one week to a full journey.
journey.lead: Use these as starting points. We can keep the retreat simple and coastal, build it around safari, or create a private program for your dates.

items.title: Private coastal reset
items.text: A compact program with morning practice, bodywork, nourishing meals, and slow afternoons by the sea.

items.title: Reset week
items.text: Daily practice, one or two workshops, optional excursions, and enough empty space to actually rest.

items.title: Practice + island immersion
items.text: A deeper week with Stone Town, spice farms, reef days, or village experiences folded gently into the rhythm.

items.title: Yoga & safari journey
items.text: The flagship route: Zanzibar practice days, five days of Tanzania safari, then back to the ocean to close.

journey.note: Our first flagship Yoga & Safari Journey departs early December 2026, while private and coastal reset formats can be shaped around your dates.

day.title: Held structure, open space.
day.lead: A typical coastal retreat day. Safari days and private programs shift the rhythm, but the balance stays the same: practice, nourishment, and unhurried time.

items.title: Dawn quiet
items.text: Optional meditation, tea, or a silent walk before the island wakes.

items.title: Morning practice
items.text: Vinyasa, hatha, or breath-led movement while the air is still cool.

items.title: Long breakfast
items.text: Fruit, fresh juice, good coffee, and time to settle into the day.

items.title: Bodywork or workshop
items.text: Thaivedic bodywork, alignment, journaling, philosophy, or a guided excursion.

items.title: Shared lunch
items.text: Plant-forward, local, and easy to adapt for dietary needs.

items.title: Ocean hours
items.text: Beach, reef, hammock, massage, or simply doing less.

items.title: Evening landing
items.text: Yin, meditation, sound, or a softer practice to close the day.

gallery.title: Moments from the mat.
gallery.lead: A glimpse of the practice — coast, terraces, warm stone, hands-on support, and the quiet after movement.

included.title: Build the retreat around the people coming.
included.lead: Every retreat quote is shaped around the teacher team, group size, room standard, meals, excursions, and whether safari is part of the route.
items: Vinyasa, hatha, yin, meditation, or Thaivedic bodywork
Boutique hotel, villa, or beach base sourcing
Optional Stone Town, reef, spice, or village excursions
Destination Paradise planning and on-island support

items.q: Is this still one retreat, or several?
items.a: Several. The flagship Yoga & Safari Journey is one retreat with full, Zanzibar-only, and safari-only participation options. We also shape coastal reset weeks and private teacher-led retreats for custom dates.
Yes. Awham explicitly teaches vinyasa for all levels, and Nina's programs can be adapted toward gentle hatha, yin, and breathwork when the group needs a softer rhythm.

For private retreats, yes. Tell us the practice style you want and we will suggest the best teacher mix, including co-led programs or guest practitioners when helpful. Fixed departures list the lead team before booking.

items.q: Can safari be added to any retreat?
items.a: Yes. Safari is built into the 14-day flagship journey, and it can be added before or after most private or coastal retreat formats.

items.q: Do we need to bring our own photos or teacher?
items.a: No. We can provide local teachers and retreat visuals, or coordinate with a teacher bringing their own group and brand material.

cta.title: Tell us the rhythm you want.
cta.text: Choose a retreat style, share your dates, and we will shape the teacher team, rooms, meals, excursions, and optional safari around your group.
cta.ai_planner: Or chat with our AI planner
